$comment: THE STYLE CONTRACT — the single source of truth that makes every SHT character look like it belongs in the same game. Change it here, once, and every future generation inherits it. This is what buys us consistency across any character we bring in.

character.$comment_mode: v3 = PixelLab's highest-quality native pixel art. Standard mode is cheaper (1 gen) but roster-inconsistent (stylized vs realistic drift). v3 = 3 gens/char and looks like our existing tokens. Worth it.
character.$comment_view: classic 3/4 iso-RPG angle. Matches the isometric combat grid. NOT 'side' (that's flat/sidescroller).
character.$comment_dir: 8 TRUE unique renders (S/SE/E/NE/N/NW/W/SW). Not mirrored — gear stays in the correct hand every facing. West faces LEFT, East faces RIGHT.
character.$comment_size: native character px; v3 canvas comes back ~244-248px. Downscaled to tokenHeight at import.
character.stylePrompt: gritty modern superhero-tactics game operative, muted realistic palette, clear readable silhouette, plain transparent background
character.$comment_style: APPENDED to every character description. This suffix is 90% of why the roster stays cohesive. Keep it stable; edit only when the whole game's look should shift.
character.negative: no purple, no lavender, no neon glow-dump, no busy background

token.$comment_tokenHeight: matches existing character_token/sprite_XX.png (~150-166px). Import trims transparent bbox then resizes to this height, nearest-neighbour to stay crisp.

tiles.$comment: Map assets share the same contract so terrain matches characters.
tiles.groundStylePrompt: gritty modern urban, muted realistic palette

objects.$comment: For inventory/gadgets/vehicles later. PixelLab create_map_object / create_8_direction_object. Same palette suffix so items match the world.
objects.itemStylePrompt: game inventory item icon, muted realistic palette, single object on transparent background, clear silhouette

ui.$comment: OWNER-LOCKED 2026-07-10: interface language is bold superhero YELLOW & BLACK (not gunmetal/amber — that was v1, rejected). Menus are RADIAL (round composable buttons, engine arranges 2-8 in a ring). Clean bold shapes only.
ui.uiStylePrompt: bold superhero yellow and black comic-book style, glossy jet black with thick vivid yellow rims, high contrast, clean bold shapes, crisp pixel art
ui.negative: no scanlines, no decorative line texture, no rivets, no segment dividers on bars, no gear emblems, no medallions, no purple
ui.color_palette: vivid superhero yellow, jet black, white highlight
ui.menus: radial — generate round icon buttons + center hub as separate pieces; engine composes the ring (see map-proto openRadial)
ui.production_note: generate BLANK buttons/plates (PixelLab letters them otherwise); text renders in-engine

grid.$comment: OWNER-LOCKED 2026-07-10: grid-based combat means items ALWAYS occupy whole grid cells, sized exactly to them. No fractional placement.
grid.$comment_cell: matches map-proto T=44. If the engine tile size changes, re-run normalize-props.mjs --cell N; footprints stay.
grid.rule: props place at integer cells; blocking footprint = cells; tall art keeps extra canvas ABOVE, anchored bottom-center
